purpose: Define the durable command/helper map layer so agents can discover safe commands, side effects, owner refs, validators, and tests without guessing.

authority_rule.schema_authority: schemas define JSON shape, required fields, allowed enum values, and portable structural constraints
authority_rule.python_validator_authority: Python validators define semantic rules, cross-file rules, side-effect rules, read-only checks, and local adoption safety
authority_rule.conflict_rule: When a schema accepts a document but the Python validator rejects it, the validator rejection blocks closeout until the schema or document is intentionally updated.

rules: Every mapped command must have a stable command_id.
Every mapped command must name a repo-relative path or explicit none.
Writer commands must be marked write_explicit and must require explicit intent.
Read-only harness commands must be compact and must not be classified as write_explicit.
Validators and builders must name at least one validator or test ref unless they are themselves the owning validator.
Generated command maps are discovery evidence, not behavior proof.
